description: A portrait-orientation employee-spotlight 'quote card' designed for Native American Heritage Month. The border is decorated with diagonal multicolored stripes (orange, teal, mustard, black, cream) evoking a Southwestern/Native textile or blanket pattern. At top, a circular photo medallion (with a layered orange/teal/black ring) shows a smiling woman with long dark hair, orange facial paint/markings on her cheeks, wearing a beaded white-and-green sash/regalia and holding what appears to be a feather fan. The center is a cream parchment-style panel with a large black quotation mark and dark serif/sans body text reading: 'Native American Heritage Month is important to me because it's a month to celebrate my culture, to bring awareness to our people and our history. There are stories of my culture that others may not fully understand, but I can bring awareness and truth about my people and the challenges we faced, not just for this month, but every single day.' Attribution in bold: 'Jessica — Immigration Service Assistant, USCIS.' The circular DHS seal appears at bottom-right. Visual style: testimonial/quote-card layout with culturally themed decorative framing and a documentary portrait. Messaging technique: humanizing the workforce and celebrating diversity/heritage; positions DHS/USCIS as inclusive. Target audience: general public and DHS workforce. A designed graphic, not a meme per se but a propaganda/PR card.
notable_text: 'Native American Heritage Month is important to me because it's a month to celebrate my culture, to bring awareness to our people and our history...' — Jessica, Immigration Service Assistant, USCIS
